tags.name: Pay Method Config Inquiry
tags.description: Pay Method Config Inquiry api description

post.description: Note: For certain Pay Methods, some optional fields are required. Kindly refer to Section Specific Pay Method Requirement

refundRequestId.description:
Refund request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check. 
(provide refundRequestId or acquiringRefundOrderId)

acquiringRefundOrderId.description:
Waffo refund order Id  
(provide refundRequestId or acquiringRefundOrderId)

AcqOrderRefundInquiry.description: A RefundOrder object will be returned

refundRequestId.description: Refund request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check.

origPaymentRequestId.description: Original Payment request id sent from Merchant.

refundStatus.description:
**REFUND_IN_PROGRESS** - refund request has been accepted and result will be sent back to merchant 
**ORDER_PARTIALLY_REFUNDED** 
**ORDER_FULLY_REFUNDED** 
**ORDER_REFUND_FAILED**

userCurrency.description: User payment currency. This is needed when merchant order currency is NOT the same as user payment currency.

finalDealAmount.description:
The final refunded amount that the user receives.
If merchant order currency is not the same as user payment currency, the finalDealAmount will not be the same as orderAmount.
The refunded amount may not include the service/tax fee that user has paid, hence the finalDealAmount may not the same as orderAmount.

remainingRefundAmount.description: Remaining Refund Amount that Merchant can still refund

userInfo.description: For certain pay method type (e.g. ONLINE_BANKING) the refund requires Merchant to provide user information to execute

RefundOrderUserInfo.description: For certain pay method type (e.g. ONLINE_BANKING) the refund requires Merchant to provide user information to execute

userEmail.description: User's email address for refund communication

userBankInfo.description:
it will include the following info in a json format: 
{ 
"bankAccountNo": "XXX", 
"bankCode": "XXX", 
"bankCity": "XXX", 
"bankBranch": "XXX" 
}

userPhone.description: Merchant user mobile phone number: +Country code-Mobile Number, for example +62-08990013157

userIDIssueDate.description: The date of issue of User's ID. Format is dd/mm/yyyy

userIDExpiryDate.description: The date of expiry of User's ID. Format is dd/mm/yyyy

FixedMaintenanceRule.description: The fixed maintenance timezone. e.g UTC+08:00, UTC+05:30

startRule.description:
The start time of fixed maintenance. written in cron expression format e.g. 
**00 00 00 ? * THU**

endRule.description:
The end time of fixed maintenance. written in cron expression format e.g. 
**00 00 00 ? * THU**

payMethodDetails.description: The array object of the pay methods details that merchant has signed a contract with

PayMethodDetail.description: The pay methods details that merchant has signed a contract with

payMethodName.description: The name of the pay method. e.g. GCash, DANA

country.description: The country of the pay method e.g. PHL, IDN

currentStatus.description:
The current availability of the pay method
- **1** - Available
- **0** - Not available

fixedMaintenanceRules.description:
The fixed maintenance period, written in cron expression format. e.g.
- **0 30 1 * * ?   -  0 30 2 * * ?** -  Not available daily from 1:30-2:30am
- **0 00 23 ? * 1,2,3,4,5  -  0 59 23 ? * 1,2,3,4,5** - Not available From Mon-Fri 11:00pm-11:59pm
- **0 00 0 1,15 * ?  -  0 59 23 1,15 * ?** - Not available on Day 1, Day 15 of the month

fixedMaintenanceTimezone.description: The fixed maintenance timezone. e.g UTC+08:00, UTC+05:30

refundRequestId.description: Refund request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check.

refundNotifyUrl.description:
The callback address to notify the merchant after the refund is completed / failed. 
If not provided, Merchant can use inquiry refund API to get the final refund status.

userInfo.description: For certain pay method type (e.g. ONLINE_BANKING) the refund requires Merchant to provide user information to execute

refundRequestId.description: Refund request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check.

refundStatus.description:
**REFUND_IN_PROGRESS** - refund request has been accepted and result will be sent back to merchant via refundNotifyUrl 
**ORDER_PARTIALLY_REFUNDED** 
**ORDER_FULLY_REFUNDED** 
**ORDER_REFUND_FAILED**

remainingRefundAmount.description: Remaining Refund Amount that Merchant can still refund

paymentRequestId.description:
Payment request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check. 
 (provide paymentRequestId or acquirerOrderId)

acquiringOrderId.description:
waffo acquiring order Id 
(provide paymentRequestId or acquirerOrderId)

goodsUrl.description:
Goods URL from merchant website , for example 
 https://www.merchant123.com/goods123 
 (for compliance and risk management purpose) 
 provide either goodsUrl or appName

appName.description:
app package name, for example com.example.app123
(for compliance and risk management purpose) 
 provide either goodsUrl or appName

AcqOrderInquiry.description: A PaymentOrder object will be returned.

paymentRequestId.description: Payment request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check.

orderStatus.description:
**PAY_IN_PROGRESS** - (merchant order is accepted, and user is in the process of payment)  
**AUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED** (pay method requires user authorization, usually for an OTP authentication or a redirection to a page for authentication)  
**AUTHED_WAITING_CAPTURE** (Order needs to be captured by merchant, applicable for CARD payment)  
**PAY_SUCCESS**  
**ORDER_CLOSE** (due to order cancel or timeout)

orderAction.description:
When orderStatus = AUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED, merchant follow the following:  
"actionType": value could be "WEB" / "DEEPLINK". If the actionType is WEB, merchant choose webUrl to redirect user. If the actionType is DEEPLINK, merchant choose deeplinkUrl to redirect user.
"webUrl": redirect user to webURL
"deeplinkUrl": redirect user to deeplink url
"actionData": {
  "paymentExpiryTime":"2023-04-01T03:00:00.000Z", (Follow ISO 8601 standard)
  "paymentQr":"xxx", (QR String Value)
  "paymentCode":"xxx",
  "paymentBarCode":"xxx",
  "paymentCurrency": "xxx",
  "paymentAmount": "xxx",
  "userFeeAmount": "xxx",
  "userFeeCurrency":"xxx",
  "exchangeRate": "xxx" (it is paymentAmount/orderAmount when orderCurrency is not the same as paymentCurrency)
}
ActionData is for those Merchants who want to build their own Cashier Page for their users, without using Waffo's cashier page

finalDealAmount.description:
The final amount that the user paid.
If merchant order currency is not the same as user payment currency, the finalDealAmount will not be the same as orderAmount. 
If there are additional service fees that the user paid, the finalDealAmount will also not be the same as orderAmount.

orderExpiredAt.description: The order expiry time set by merchant. If not set, the default merchant contract expiry time will be used

cancelRedirectUrl.description: The address of the merchant page to redirect to when user cancels the payment

userCurrency.description: User payment currency. This is needed when merchant order currency is NOT the same as user payment currency

userCountryCode.description: The country code of the source of the user request

userTerminal.description:
user terminal type: 
- **WEB** (user is using a PC) 
- **APP** (user is using a Mobile Phone) 
- **IN_WALLET_APP** (user is inside a pay method's wallet app (e.g. Wechat app)) 
- **IN_MINI_PROGRAM** (user is inside a pay method's mini program (e.g. Wechat mini program)) 
Not all pay methods support the above user terminals, hence such info is used for checking if a user can use a particular pay method for his/her payment

userBrowserIp.description: Merchant user browser IP address. This is field is only required when merchant pass in card/applepay/googlepay data directly in API

userAgent.description: Merchant user browser agent. This is field is only required when merchant pass in card/applepay/googlepay data directly in API

payMethodName.description:
The actual payment method. e.g. DANA, GCASH
If payMethodName is provided, the payment request will be processed using this pay method directly. 
If payMethodName is not provided, user will be redirected to Waffo's cashier page to choose the final pay method. 
If merchant intends to limit the card payment scheme, e.g. Restrict user to use only VISA card, merchant can specify payMethodName as CC_VISA, DC_VISA

payMethodProperties.description:
For certain pay methods, there could be certain specific info needed. Json format e.g. {"key1":"value1", "key2":"value2"}. Currently support the following key: 
- cpf 
- cardPrefix 
cpf: specify the user's cpf number used in this payment
cardPrefix: restrict user card payment by a prefix card bin number, from 4-9 digits. Multiple card bins can be separated by commas, with a maximum of 80 allowed.
cardPrefixPurpose: input restriction purpose. Merchant can set as restriction or promotion. Waffo will display the respective error message to the user

payMethodResponse.description:
The specific information returned by Pay Method 
Json format 
{ 
"payMethodRefId": "xxx",  
"exchangeRate": "xxx",  
"userOpenId": "xxx" 
} 
- payMethodRefId: This is the payment ref ID that user often sees in its transaction history from Pay Method's app/portal 
- exchangeRate: this is the exchange rate used by Pay Method during user payment
- userOpenId: this is the unique user open ID assigned by the pay method

payMethodUserAccountNo.description: User account number at the designated pay method

payMethodUserAccountType.description:
User account type at the designated pay method 
EMAIL 
PHONE_NO 
ACCOUNT_ID

payOption.description:
The pay option user has selected, for example
- **BALANCE** 
- **DEBIT_CARD** 
- **CREDIT_CARD** 
If the pay method does not support users to select the pay option, the field will be empty

cashierLanguage.description:
Merchant specify the cashier language. It follows IETF BCP 47 language tag
The format is Language (two-letter language code from ISO 639-1), follow by an optional script subtag, based on a four-letter script code from ISO 15924, and follow by an optional region subtag based on a two-letter country code from ISO 3166-1 alpha-2
For example: 
- en-HK: English in Hong Kong
- zh-Hant-HK: Traditional Chinese in Hong Kong
- zh-Hans-HK: Simplified Chinese in Hong Kong

payMethodCountry.description: Some pay methods are available in multiple countries (e.g. GrabPay, ShoppeePay), the merchant shall specify the pay method country when necessarily

merchantInitiatedMode.description:
Specify when it is a merchant initiated transaction (mit) without customer involvement. userPaymentAccessToken is also required for mit transaction
- scheduled
- unscheduled

subMerchantId.description: Sub merchant id assigned by Waffo

period.description: Which period the subscription payment belong to. For example 2

merchantRequest.description: Subscription request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check.

cardExpiryYear.description: User's card expiry year, the format is four digits. For example 2025

cardExpiryMonth.description: User's card expiry year, the format is two digits. For example 10 (October)

threeDsDecision.description:
Merchant's 3DS decision. Merchant can provide its own 3DS decision. The following 3DS Decision are supported: +
            - 3DS_FORCE
            - 3DS_ATTEMPT
            - NO_3DS
            However, the eventual 3DS decision is also subject to Waffo's risk decision.

paymentRequestId.description: Payment request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check.

userCurrency.description: User payment currency. This is needed when merchant order currency is NOT the same as user payment currency

orderExpiredAt.description: The order expiry time set by merchant. If not set, the default merchant contract expiry time will be used

successRedirectUrl.description:
The address of the merchant page to redirect to after successful payment. 
If no value is provided, it will stay on the cashier page after successful payment.

failedRedirectUrl.description: The address of the merchant page to which the payment pre-verification fails, or the redirection after the payment fails. If no value is provided, it will stay on the cashier page after the payment fails

cancelRedirectUrl.description: The address of the merchant page to redirect to when user cancels the payment

notifyUrl.description: The callback address to notify the merchant after the payment is completed / failed

cardInfo.description: Card data info. It is only required when Merchant wants to pass in card data directly in API (note: Merchant needs to have PCI DSS certification)

paymentTokenData.description: User's ApplePay / GooglePay payment token. It is only required when Merchant choose to integrate with ApplePay / GooglePay frontend directly and pass in the encrypted payment token for Waffo to process the payment

userType.description: Merchant user: Individual / Agent / Institution / Internal

userLegalName.description: Merchant user legal name (or first name + last name)

userLastSeenIp.description: Merchant user last login IP address

userIsNew.description: Merchant user if he/she is a new user: Yes / No

userIsFirstPurchase.description: Merchant user if this is his/her first time purchase: Yes / No

subMerchantId.description: Sub merchant id assigned by Waffo

paymentRequestId.description: Payment request id sent from Merchant.

orderStatus.description:
Order status 
**PAY_IN_PROGRESS** -(merchant order is accepted, and user is in the process of payment) 
**AUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED** (pay method requires user authorization, merchant needs to bring user to the authorization page by retrieving the information from orderAction) 
**AUTHED_WAITING_CAPTURE** (Order needs to be captured by merchant, applicable for CARD payment) 
 **PAY_SUCCESS** 
**ORDER_CLOSE** (due to order cancel, failed or timeout)

orderAction.description:
When orderStatus = AUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED, merchant follow the following:  
"**actionType**": value could be "WEB" / "DEEPLINK". If the actionType is WEB, merchant choose webUrl to redirect user. If the actionType is DEEPLINK, merchant choose deeplinkUrl to redirect user.
"**webUrl**": redirect user to webURL
"**deeplinkUrl**": redirect user to deeplink url
"actionData": {
  "paymentExpiryTime":"2023-04-01T03:00:00.000Z", (Follow ISO 8601 standard)
  "paymentQr":"xxx", (QR String Value)
  "paymentCode":"xxx",
  "paymentBarCode":"xxx",
  "paymentCurrency": "xxx",
  "paymentAmount": "xxx",
  "userFeeAmount": "xxx",
  "userFeeCurrency":"xxx",
  "exchangeRate": "xxx" (it is paymentAmount/orderAmount when orderCurrency is not the same as paymentCurrency)
}
ActionData is for those Merchants who want to build their own Cashier Page for their users, without using Waffo's cashier page

paymentRequestId.description:
Payment request id sent from Merchant. It will also be used for idempotent check. 
(provide paymentRequestId or acquirerOrderId)

acquiringOrderId.description:
Waffo acquiring order Id  
(provide paymentRequestId or acquirerOrderId)

paymentRequestId.description: Payment request id sent from Merchant.

orderStatus.description: **ORDER_CLOSE** (due to order cancel, failed or timeout)

totalDailyLimit.description:
Daily transaction limit setting. 
 If there is a daily limit, it will be returned as json format as `{"currencyCode1":"value1", "currencyCode2":"value2"}`
 For example, 
`{"PHP":"100000", "IDR":"2000000"}`
If there is no limit setting, it will be empty

remainingDailyLimit.description:
Remaining Daily transaction limit 
If there is a remaining daily limit, it will be returned as json format as 
`{"currencyCode1":"value1", "currencyCode2":"value2"}` 
For example, 
`{"PHP":"10000", "IDR":"1000000"}`
If there is no limit setting, it will be empty

transactionLimit.description:
Single transaction limit setting. 
If there is a limit setting, it will be returned as json format as 
`{"currencyCode1":"value1", "currencyCode2":"value2"}` 
For example, 
`{"PHP":"5000", "IDR":"100000"}`
If there is no limit set, it will be empty
